tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly nephrotic syndrome or acute/chronic kidney disease. Hyperkalemia and hyperchloremia further support renal dysfunction. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, high protein, ketones, bilirubin) reinforce renal pathology. HbA1c is low, excluding diabetes as a cause. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for further assessment and management.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
